story: Existing Amplitude customer: 2K Games expansion ($350K new ARR) sits within the broader Take-Two Interactive relationship ($137.7K total ARR, $127.5K ATR). Analytically underutilized: event volume query coverage at 37% vs. 83% healthy benchmark, and MAU declining from a peak of ~47 in Jul '25 to ~30 today.

Core pain: Treasure Data CDP severely underperforming, limiting campaigns to 1–3/quarter vs. the industry standard of ~1/week; data rolled up into just 4 events, blocking meaningful user journey analysis and self-service insights for game teams.

Compelling event: AI transformation at 2K is creating a once-in-a-cycle window to modernize the analytics stack — Christian Yoder (Bioshock/Borderlands Core BU) is driving a two-phase AI POC with a hard Q2 close target; April 17 AI Overview demo is the pivotal next milestone.

2K's own team called Amplitude 'clearly the best for user experience' and 'best in class' — expansion opportunity surfaced through Core BU engagement with 50–90 additional campaigns/year possible and direct monetization revenue upside.

techStack.cdp: Treasure Data (underperforming, effectively EOL for this use case)

threeWhys.whyAnything: 2K's existing analytics workflow supports only 4 live campaigns/month at ~40 hours of setup each. Their Treasure Data CDP has collapsed as an operational tool, the data team is overloaded, and game teams can't act on insights without engineering help. Without a change, they'll keep missing monetization windows and watching campaigns launch weeks late.
threeWhys.whyNow: The AI transformation already underway at 2K creates a once-in-a-cycle window to modernize the analytics stack alongside new instrumentation — Christian Yoder is driving evaluation to close this quarter, the AWS co-sell path simplifies procurement, and the April 17 AI demo is a hard inflection point. Delaying means another quarter of lost campaign velocity.
threeWhys.whyAmplitude: Amplitude wins 87% of head-to-head matchups with Mixpanel and 2K's own team called us 'clearly best for user experience.' Our AI agents answer the 'why' questions Mixpanel admits they can't address. The Databricks warehouse integration complements — rather than replaces — Christian's existing stack, and the Steam user feedback integration directly outperforms CleverTap in gaming-specific use cases.

metrics.evidence: Buyer-validated: ~4 campaigns/month today → 50–90/year possible; ~40hrs/campaign setup → 50–65% faster execution; direct revenue impact from earlier monetization campaign launches. Data team bandwidth costs quantified as blocking roadmap.
metrics.gap: ROI/Value Map deliverable (Ali Bhamani) not yet finalized — needed for exec call 5/4.

economicBuyer.evidence: Nibeta Baral (CTO) confirmed as EB via Stage 2 qualification field in Salesforce. Exec call with Nate Crook targeting 5/4.
economicBuyer.gap: Direct EB engagement not yet achieved — Nibeta not yet in the conversation. Nate Crook prep and exec call 5/4 is critical path.

decisionCriteria.evidence: Criteria documented in SF: measurable uplift in monetization/retention, campaign velocity, data security/governance, cross-functional adoption. 2K confirmed Amplitude is 'best in class' on UX.
decisionCriteria.gap: Criteria not formally confirmed with EB (Nibeta Baral). Value Map needed to tie criteria to quantified outcomes.

decisionProcess.evidence: Two-phase approach documented: internal POC (Christian driving) → socialized POC with select customers → exec steering (Luke Whelan) → decision. Goal is Q2 close.
decisionProcess.gap: Luke Whelan not yet formally engaged. Shortlisting process unclear — may still include Mixpanel. Mutual Action Plan not in place.

paperProcess.evidence: Existing MSA in place (Amplitude is already a vendor). Pricing discussion to be scheduled week of 4/20. 2K to provide competitive pricing guidance.
paperProcess.gap: Security review not initiated. Paper process not started. Luke Whelan is a key exec steering stakeholder for final approval — not yet engaged.

identifiedPain.evidence: Extensively documented across Salesforce, Granola, and Slack: Treasure Data CDP failure limiting campaigns to 1–3/quarter, data rolled up to 4 events blocking user journey analysis, bandwidth constraints on data team, slow time-to-insight creating analyst dependency.
identifiedPain.gap: Pain formally validated with champion but not yet tied to an EB-level business case narrative.

champion.evidence: Dhruv Bhargava listed in SF as Champion. Ed Lee is the active POC driver and most engaged stakeholder. Kit Brown-Watts is a strong internal coach. Christian Yoder owns the technical evaluation.
champion.gap: Champion (Ed Lee / Dhruv) hasn't been formally tested — would they buy if it were their decision? Two-phase socialization still underway. POD hasn't confirmed champion strength.

competition.evidence: Mixpanel (incumbent, broadly used across 2K properties), In-House Infrastructure, Tableau, CleverTap all identified. Amplitude wins 87% vs. Mixpanel; AI agents directly address Mixpanel's stated gap. CleverTap compete materials in hand.
competition.gap: Competitive shortlist not confirmed with EB. 'Other' vendor in SF may indicate additional competitors not yet surfaced.

whereWeAreStuck: Data instrumentation too basic for full POC depth — data rolled up to 4 events blocks meaningful evaluation; Christian is bandwidth-constrained to fix (AI initiatives competing for his time)
EB (Nibeta Baral) not yet directly engaged — exec call is 5/4 which is a late-stage risk if AI demo on 4/17 doesn't create urgency
6sense buying stage regressed Purchase → Decision on 4/13 — intent signal weakening going into demo week
